content: I there any opportunities to contribute to the unit for the new rust code in linux ?
3 messages were moved here from #General > bindings docs by Boqun Feng.
I should think so: kuint support has been proposed. Unit tests will be helpful.
Thanks for the link. Are we writing kunit test for the new modules ? How is the new modules in the rust dir tested ?

This is no definite rule right now, but if kernel unit tests can be written similar to userspace Rust tests, for any module in kernel written by Rust, there is no excuse for not having unit tests. ;-)

So if you can download the current kernel source, read the Rust code, and find out unit tests are needed, your contribution is welcome ;-) Of course, you will need to make sure the tests are valuable and you have tested them yourself.

I am trying to write a unit test for my bitmap patch. It adds a Rust API over the C bitmap functions. When I write a #[test], I get unresolved symbols at link time. My code obviously needs some functions from bitmap.o - do I need to modify the Makefile around the rusttesttarget? Or was this supposed to be working automagically? @Miguel Ojeda
Wait, I have been using #[cfg(test)] and probably should have used #[kunit_tests(rust_bitmap)] instead.
rusttest is unrelated to the KUnit #[kunit...] tests.

What I would like to do (but not sure whether it makes sense): add a simple #[test] target for unit testing that runs on the host. Though the Rust lib does depend on a kernel library (specifically bitmap.o).

OK, instead of #[cfg(test)] I was able to run my test with #[kunit_tests(rust_kernel_kunit)].
Thinking aloud: what I wanted to do with #[cfg(test)] is probably nonsensical, because #[cfg(test)] which are run by rusttest are userland tests, and for those, depending on kernel C libs is not something that can work in general.

Yeah, exactly. If you depend on any kernel code, then you need to run in the kernel, and thus KUnit.

rusttest was the initial thing we had, when we didn't have the KUnit integration. And probably the few userspace tests that we have there could/should be migrated to KUnit anyway at this point now that we got the #[kunit...] support. We may reuse the target for other things in the future, though.
